summary: Re-review: bird-001 and bird-002 RESOLVED by bert. All 4 findings closed. No open BLOCKs or WARNs. David sign-off on wording still required (product/copy decision, not a UX gate).

steps.factual_accuracy: done — new text correct; old text was wrong
steps.length_overflow_check: done — replacement text within sibling range
steps.viewports_checked: skipped — copy-only change, no layout impact
steps.branding_check: done — re-review confirms both WARNs resolved
steps.re_review: done — diff read; bert applied exact recommended phrasing

findings.claim: Opening noun 'items' broke sibling pattern; all other sections use 'tasks'
findings.evidence: Re-review: new text opens 'These tasks have a past scheduled date...' — matches all sibling help texts. RESOLVED by bert.

findings.claim: 'committed items' was unexplained scheduler jargon; violated plain-language brand voice
findings.evidence: Re-review: 'committed items' replaced with 'fixed-time tasks and calendar-linked events stay pinned at their original date' — plain English, no internal vocabulary. RESOLVED by bert.

findings.claim: Missing actionable close — siblings consistently end with what the user can do
findings.evidence: Re-review: 'Reschedule manually when you're ready.' appended — actionable close restored. RESOLVED by bert.

findings.claim: Minor grammar roughness: plural subject with singular 'their date'
findings.evidence: Re-review: 'their date' replaced with 'their original date'. RESOLVED by bert.
